decks.name: Types of Plants
decks.description: Learn about different types of plants around us

cards.front: What are the three main types of plants based on their size?
cards.back: Trees, shrubs, and herbs. Trees are the tallest with thick trunks, shrubs are medium-sized with many branches, and herbs are small and soft.
cards.hint: Think of big, medium, and small plants

cards.back: A herb is a small plant with a soft, green stem. Examples include mint, coriander, and wheat. Herbs are usually short and live for one or two seasons.
cards.hint: Think of the small plants in your kitchen garden

cards.back: A shrub is a medium-sized plant with many branches growing close to the ground. Examples include rose, hibiscus, and tulsi. Shrubs have thin, woody stems.
cards.hint: These plants are bigger than herbs but smaller than trees

cards.back: Climbers are plants that grow upward by holding onto a support, like money plant and grapevine. Creepers are plants that spread along the ground, like pumpkin and watermelon.
cards.hint: Some plants need help standing up, others spread on the ground

cards.front: What makes a tree different from a shrub?
cards.back: A tree has one thick, strong, woody trunk and is very tall. A shrub has many thin stems branching near the ground and is shorter than a tree.

decks.name: Uses of Plants
decks.description: Discover the many ways plants help us every day

cards.front: How do plants give us food?
cards.back: Different parts of plants give us food. We eat roots (carrot), stems (sugarcane), leaves (spinach), flowers (broccoli), fruits (mango), and seeds (rice, wheat).
cards.hint: Think about which part of the plant you eat

cards.front: How do plants help us breathe?
cards.back: Plants take in carbon dioxide and release oxygen during the day. Oxygen is the gas we need to breathe. Without plants, there would not be enough oxygen for us.
cards.hint: Think about the air around you

cards.front: Name some useful things we get from trees.
cards.back: We get wood for furniture and houses, rubber for tyres and erasers, paper from wood pulp, fruits, shade, and many medicines from trees.
cards.hint: Look around your home for things made from trees

cards.front: How do plants help the soil?
cards.back: Plant roots hold the soil tightly and stop it from being washed away by rain or blown away by wind. This is called preventing soil erosion.
cards.hint: Think about what plant roots do underground

cards.back: Medicinal plants are plants used to treat illnesses. Examples include tulsi (for cough and cold), neem (for skin problems), and aloe vera (for burns and skin care).
cards.hint: Some plants can help when you are sick
